blurb: Apple platforms support TLS 1.2 and 1.3 with forward-secrecy cipher suites mandated by App Transport Security. Preferred cipher suites include ECDHE with AES-256-GCM, AES-128-GCM, and ChaCha20-Poly1305, with SHA-2 family hash functions for integrity. SSLv3, RC4, and 3DES are explicitly disabled. Certificate validation includes full chain verification to trusted roots, OCSP stapling for revocation checking, and Certificate Transparency enforcement requiring signed certificate timestamps from qualified CT logs. ATS additionally requires a minimum RSA key size of 2048 bits or ECC keys of at least 256 bits, blocking weak legacy configurations.
